title: Audio pipeline: transcode + EBU R128 normalise + checksums + derived formats
context: The corpus requires a reproducible audio processing pipeline that takes submitted audio, validates it against the CI consent and license gates, normalises loudness to the EBU R128 standard (approximately -23 LUFS), preserves the archival lossless original, generates delivery-format derivatives, and stores integrity checksums. The pipeline is the technical backbone of M1 data ingestion. It must refuse any input lacking a valid ConsentRecord or referencing an unapproved source. Depends on place-name-pronunciation-ci-001 for the gate checks.
objective: Implement an ffmpeg-based audio processing pipeline CLI that: validates input against the CI consent and license gates; preserves archival FLAC/WAV; generates derived OGG Opus and MP3; normalises loudness to EBU R128 (-23 LUFS target); computes and stores SHA-256 checksums per artifact; and records duration and loudness in the Recording metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: Pipeline refuses (exits non-zero) any audio input that lacks a valid ConsentRecord or references an unapproved source.
Archival lossless copy (FLAC or WAV) is preserved unchanged alongside derived formats.
OGG Opus and MP3 delivery formats are generated from the archival source.
Loudness is normalised to EBU R128 target (~-23 LUFS); measured LUFS value is stored in the Recording metadata.
SHA-256 checksum is computed and stored for each generated artifact (archival + each derived format).
Duration (seconds) is recorded per Recording via ffprobe.
Pipeline is wired into pnpm build/test/lint and CI passes.
Maintainer reviews and merges the PR.
output: Audio pipeline CLI package (TypeScript/ESM) committed via PR with passing CI, normalising audio to EBU R128 and generating archival + derived formats with checksums.
